Q6 MCQ Not checked
General Aptitude • ELECTRICAL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

A person starts typing the numbers from 1 to 1999. He press the keys total ‘n’ number of times.
The value of n is

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
The number of times he pressed the keys for one digit number = 9
The number of times he pressed the keys for two digit numbers = 90 × 2 = 180
The numbers of times he pressed the keys for three digit numbers = 900 × 3 = 2700
The number of times he pressed the keys for the numbers 1000 to 1999 is 1000 × 4 = 4000
Thus, total number of times the keys are pressed = 6889

Q8 MCQ Not checked
General Aptitude • ELECTRICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Below is a sentence of which some parts have been jumbled up. Rearrange these parts which are labelled P, Q, R and S to produce the correct sentence. Choose the proper sequence. It would
P : that they were quite in the dark
Q : about the policy of management
R : appear from their statement
S : in dealing with the strike
The proper sequence should be

Correct answer: A
Solution

(b)
“It would appear ...” can be the only coherent beginning of the sentence. Thus the first part
would be R. Since R says “their statement”, sentence P would follow it because it also says, “that they ...”
Thus, option (b) is the correct answer.

Q9 MSQ Not checked
General Aptitude • ELECTRICAL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

If you are a brisk walker, there is no need to go to a gym. Your neighbourhood can become your gym. You don’t need fancy equipment to get a good exercise either. All you need is a welldesigned pair of shoes.
The paragraph best supports the statement that

Correct answer: D, C, B, A
Solution

(d)
By stating that brisk walking does not require commute to a gym, the author stresses the
convenience of this form of exercise. The paragraph also states that brisk walking may result in a good workout.
Option (c) is not appropriate. A gym membership can be a good investment for some.
Option (b) is not given in the passage.
Option (a) seems logical but the paragraph does not mention it.
Option (d) is the most appropriate option.

Q13 MCQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science & Artificial Intelligence • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Consider the following Python code:

 
What will be the output when this code is executed?
Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
Condition 1:
s1.lower( ) == s2.lower( )
s1.lower( ) → "madeeasy2026"
s2.lower( ) → "madeeasy2026"
Both are equal → Condition 1 is True
Condition 2:
s1.isupper( ) != s2.isupper( )
s1.isupper( ) → "MADEEASY2026" → all letters are uppercase → True
s2.isupper( ) → "MADEeasy2026" → contains lowercase → False
The if condition requires both conditions to be true: True AND True → True
So, the program executes the first print statement.
Gate Exam Qualified.

Which linkage method is typically more sensitive to outliers because it relies on the maximum distance between clusters?

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
Complete linkage is determined by the farthest pair of points. An outlier far away from the group
can significantly increase the ‘distance’ of that cluster, delaying its merger.

Q26 MCQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science & Artificial Intelligence • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Consider the following statements:
S1 : Z-distribution is symmetric about the mean.
S2 : A T-distribution with 1 degree of freedom is symmetric about the mean.
S3 : A Chi-squared distribution with very large degrees of freedom (approaching infinity) is approximately symmetric about the mean.
Which of the following statements is correct?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
• The Z-distribution is a standard normal distribution. It is perfectly bell-shaped and symmetric
about its mean (0). So, S1 is correct.
• A T-distribution with 1 degree of freedom is heavily tailed and not reliably symmetric for
practical purposes. Its variance is infinite, so S2 is considered incorrect. So, S2 is incorrect.
• Chi-squared distribution is right-skewed for small degrees of freedom. However, as the
degrees of freedom increase, it approaches normality and becomes approximately symmetric.
So, S3 is correct.

Q32 MCQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science & Artificial Intelligence • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

The context of Gibbs Sampling, which statement accurately describes the requirement for the order in which variables are updated to ensure convergence to the stationary distribution?

Correct answer: B
Solution

(c)
• Gibbs Sampling requires that every variable must be updated infinitely often (i.e., given enough
iterations, every variable will eventually be sampled from its full conditional distribution).
• The update order can follow a fixed sequence (systematic scan) or a random sequence (random
scan).
• Both the fixed and random scan approaches are valid and lead to the same target stationary
distribution.
• Option (d) is incorrect because Gibbs Sampling is a sequential, single-site updating method,
not a simultaneous one.

Q41 MCQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science & Artificial Intelligence • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Depth-Limited Search (DLS) with depth limit L is executed on a search tree where:
• the tree is infinite
• branching factor is infinite at some levels
• the goal is at depth ≤ L
Consider the statements:
S1 : DLS may still be incomplete even if the goal lies within the depth limit.
S2 : DLS becomes complete only if both depth and branching factor are finite.
Which of the above is/are correct?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
• Even if a goal exists at depth ≤ L, an infinite branching factor at a shallower depth can cause
DLS to explore an infinite subtree before reaching the goal → incomplete. So, S1 is correct.
• DLS is complete only when
(i) the tree depth is finite
(ii) the branching factor is finite.
for guaranteed completeness.
So, S2 is correct.

Q51 MCQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science & Artificial Intelligence • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider the following function that takes reference to head of a double linked list as parameter.
Assume that a node of doubly linked list has previous pointer as prev and next pointer as next
class Node:

 
 
Assume that reference of head of the following doubly linked list is passed to above function 11 ↔ 22 ↔ 33 ↔ 44 ↔ 55 ↔ 66.
What should be the modified linked list after the function call?
 
Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
The function swaps prev and next for every node, reversing the links.
Traversal uses current = current.prev because pointers are swapped.
After the loop, temp.prev becomes the new head.
Hence, the head becomes 66 and all nodes are reversed.
The final list is 66 ↔ 55 ↔ 44 ↔ 33 ↔ 22 ↔ 11.

Q53 MSQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science & Artificial Intelligence • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Which of the following statements is/are correct?

Correct answer: B, C
Solution

(b, c)
• Shuffling is recommended, especially for ordered data, but not a requirement.
K-Fold can still run even without shuffling, though accuracy may be biased. So, option (a) is
incorrect.
• When K is small, each fold has less training data, causing the performance estimate to become
more biased.
This is a well-known bias-variance behavior of K-Fold. So, option (b) is correct.
• K-Fold averages performance across multiple train-test splits.
This reduces dependence on any single random split, giving a more stable estimate. So, option
(c) is correct.
• Hold-Out uses only one train-test split and trains the model only once.
Training K times is done in K-Fold, not in Hold-Out. So, option (d) is incorrect.

Solution

(b)

 
Q55 MCQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science & Artificial Intelligence • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Consider the following statements regarding the K-Means clustering algorithm:
S1 : The K-Means objective function is guaranteed to decrease (or remain unchanged) after each update of cluster assignments and centroid positions.
S2 : In K-Means clustering, increasing the value of K always results in a decrease in the value of the objective function.
Which of the above statements is/are correct?

Correct answer: A
Solution

(c)
• K-Means alternates between assigning points to their nearest centroid and recomputing centroids. Both steps reduce (or preserve) the within-cluster sum of squared distances. So, S1 is correct.
• As K increases, clusters become smaller and each point is closer to its centroid. Therefore, the
objective function (WCSS) always decreases with larger K. So, S2 is correct.

A max-heap is a heap where the value of each parent is greater than or equal to the values of its children. How many max-heaps is/are there in below figure?

Correct answer: 2
Solution

2
I. Not a max heap because level-2 is not filled completely before level-3.
II. This is a max heap.
III. Not a max heap because the value of node (5) is less than its child node (8).
III. This is a max heap.

Q61 MCQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science & Artificial Intelligence • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider a database that has a relation schema:

 
 
Assume that every student is enrolled in at least one course, then which of the following query
“Retrieve the names of students who have scored grade ‘A’ in all subjects they have enrolled”?
Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
In option (b)
When Student e with all A grade, for enrollment tuples not having her roll number, LHS is false.
For enrollment tuples having her/his roll number, LHS is true, RHS also true so the implication is
true for all e tuples.
When Student e with some non-S grades, for enrollment tuples not having her roll number,
LHS is false.
For enrollment tuples having her roll number, LHS is true, but RHS is false for at least one tuple.

Q65 MSQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science & Artificial Intelligence • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Which of the following statements is/are correct?

Correct answer: A, B, C
Solution

(a, b, c)
(a) A star schema uses one fact table linked to several denormalized dimension tables. The structure
supports fast query performance and efficient aggregation for analytical tasks. Its simplicity
reduces join complexity, making it ideal for OLAP. So, option (a) is correct.
(b) Roll-up aggregates values from a lower level to a higher level, such as days → months
→ years. It helps in viewing summarized patterns and reduces the level of granularity. So,
option (b) is correct.
(c) OLTP systems handle frequent inserts, updates, and deletes with low latency. They ensure
consistency and concurrency for business transactions. Thus, they are not intended for analytical
workloads. So, option (c) is correct.
(d) Star schemas intentionally use denormalized dimension tables. Denormalization improves
performance by reducing the number of joins needed in OLAP queries. Therefore, fully
normalizing them would defeat the purpose of a star schema. So, option (d) is incorrect.
